chapter_title.ar: بَابُ مَنْ بَدَأَ بِالْمَرْوَةِ قَبْلَ الصَّفَا أَوْ سَهَا فِي السَّعْيِ بَيْنَهُمَا
chapter_title.en: Chapter 143 – The one who begins with Al-Marwa before Al-Safa, or makes a mistake in the Sa’ee between the two

gradings: Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: مجهول - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (18/75)
Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: مجهول - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (18/75)
text: سُئِلَ أَبُو عَبْدِ اللَّهِ ( عليه السلام ) وَ أَنَا حَاضِرٌ عَنْ رَجُلٍ بَدَأَ بِالْمَرْوَةِ قَبْلَ الصَّفَا قَالَ يُعِيدُ أَ لَا تَرَى أَنَّهُ لَوْ بَدَأَ بِشِمَالِهِ قَبْلَ يَمِينِهِ كَانَ عَلَيْهِ أَنْ يَبْدَأَ بِيَمِينِهِ ثُمَّ يُعِيدَ عَلَى شِمَالِهِ .

en.hubeali: Ali Bin Ibrahim, from his father, from Ismail Bin Marrar, from Yunus, from Ali Al Sani’e who said,

‘Abu Abdullah<sup>asws</sup> was asked and I was present, about a man who began with Al-Marwa before Al-Safa. He<sup>asws</sup> said: ‘He should repeat. Do you not see that had he began (his ablution) with his left hand before his right hand, upon him would be that he begins with his right hand, then return upon his left hand?’
en.sarwar: 4. Ali ibn Ibrahim has narrated from his father from ‘Isma‘il ibn Marrar from Yunus from Ali al-Sa‘igh who has said the following: “Abu ‘Abd Allah, ‘Alayhi al-Salam, was asked, when I was present, about a man who begins from al- Marwah instead of from al-Safa’. He (the Imam) said, ‘He must do it again. Consider if you begin with your left arms for wudu’ instead of with your right arm, you must begin with your right arm and then do your left arm again.’”
